snippet: This glossary is intended to assist the users of this document in understanding the terms that have been used throughout this document. Ad-hoc travel A student that requires occasional travel may be considered as ad-hoc. Ad-hoc travel can be used for, but not limited to, sports training, part time work, social travel to fellow students' residence and so on. Ad-hoc travel is at the coordinating principal’s discretion to approve or decline. Bus operator For the purposes of the School Bus Program, a bus operator is a company or sole trader who is contracted by the Department of Transport and Planning (DTP) to operate a school bus service along a specified route. Bus operators must be accredited with Transport Safety Victoria (TSV) and meet safety obligations under the Bus Safety Act 2009. Client school A client school is a school whose students use school buses provided under the School Bus Program but the school does not manage the bus service (this is the responsibility of the coordinating school).
